title: Model file separation refactor
summary: Refactor parent DB models into sensible per-table files, lock vocabulary decisions into repo docs, and add structural regression coverage so unrelated models are never dumped into one generic file again.

items.description: Inventory the current model layout, identify every table/model currently living in generic or overloaded files, and produce the exact target file map for the split starting from the current single-module `app/db/models/auth.py` reality.
steps: Search the repo for all SQLAlchemy model classes and current model module files.
Record the current overloaded-module state, including the exact model classes still defined in `app/db/models/auth.py`.
List each current table/model and the file it lives in.
Propose the exact target file path for each model after refactor.
Record any blockers such as circular imports or metadata-registration risks.
pass_criteria: The progress log contains a discovery summary mapping current model locations to target files, including the current `auth.py` source module for each table.
No runtime code behavior is changed in this item.

items.description: Add the locked vocabulary decision record to the repo and align architecture docs to reference it as the canonical source for parent-site enums/status values.
steps: Create the vocabulary decision record using the locked values provided by John.
Update repo docs to tell future runs not to invent alternate vocabularies.
Update AGENTS.md if needed to enforce the new vocabulary source.
pass_criteria: The vocabulary decision record exists and matches the locked decisions.
Repo control docs reference it explicitly.

items.description: Split identity/auth/account models out of any generic file into per-table modules with sensible names.
steps: Move each identity/auth table model into its own file.
Keep metadata and table names unchanged unless the current spec explicitly requires otherwise.
Update imports and model package exports cleanly.
pass_criteria: Each identity/auth model lives in its own sensible file.

items.description: Split profile/settings/address/integration models into per-table modules with sensible names.
steps: Move each profile/settings/address/integration table model into its own file.
Use the locked vocabulary decision record for any enum/status fields touched during the refactor.
Update imports and exports cleanly.
pass_criteria: Each profile/settings/address/integration model is in its own sensible file.

items.description: Split support/content/status models into per-table modules with sensible names.
steps: Move each support/content/status table model into its own file.
Ensure attachment-related status fields use the locked scan-status vocabulary.
Update imports and exports cleanly.
pass_criteria: Each support/content/status model is in its own sensible file.

items.description: Split billing/read-model projection models into per-table modules with sensible names.
steps: Move each billing/read-model projection table into its own file.
Use canonical Pay-mirrored vocabularies where the decision record requires exact reuse.
Keep the payment-method summary status separate from the `is_default` boolean.
pass_criteria: Each billing/read-model projection model is in its own sensible file.

items.description: Align model package imports, Base metadata registration, and Alembic discovery after the file split while preserving the existing bootstrap path through `app/db/bootstrap.py`.
steps: Ensure all model modules are imported for metadata registration.
Confirm Alembic still sees the full metadata set.
Avoid circular-import regressions.
pass_criteria: Model metadata registration still works after the split.
The authoritative docker test suite is green.

items.description: Add structural regression tests that guard against future model-dump regressions and verify the intended model module layout is preserved.
steps: Add a test that verifies expected model modules exist and are importable.
Add a test that verifies metadata contains the expected tables after model imports.
Do not rely on brittle exact file-count assertions if the project may legitimately grow.
pass_criteria: Structural regression tests exist and pass.
The tests would fail if unrelated models were collapsed back into one file without proper registration.

items.description: Run the authoritative docker test suite and mark the spec complete only after the full suite is green.
steps: Run the authoritative docker test command.
Record the exact result in progress/progress.txt.
Only then mark the spec complete.
pass_criteria: The authoritative docker suite exits green.
All prior items in this spec are complete.
